whatsNew: Motion is now scoped by surface. A blanket 'no scroll-linked / no parallax / no 3D / no animated gradients' was right for product surfaces (dashboards, data, forms — keep them calm) but wrong for the exact brand pages StyleSeed points to as best-in-class (family.co, stripe.com, linear.app) — those pages ARE scroll-choreographed. New Cinematic tier: on a marketing/landing/brand page, scroll-LINKED reveals, pinned sections, the 'product assembles as you scroll' move, subtle parallax, a 3D/tilt hero, animated gradient/video backgrounds, and rich hover are explicitly allowed — with guardrails (60fps transform/opacity only, never blocks the first read or the CTA, prefers-reduced-motion leaves a complete static page, one motion language). The distinction the old rule blurred: scroll-JACKING (hijacking scroll, trapping) stays banned everywhere; scroll-LINKED (native scroll drives it, user in control) is encouraged on brand pages. App surfaces keep the restraint — the bans got a surface, they didn't go away.
update: Run /ss-update, or: npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed
